title: US company ClickUp lays off 22% workforce, but CEO says bigger rewards await those...
description: ClickUp Layoffs: ClickUp is cutting 22 per cent of its workforce as the company shifts more aggressively towards artificial intelligence-driven operations. CEO Zeb Evans said the move is part of a broader plan to achieve “100x output” using AI tools and automation. He also stated that employees who deliver exceptional results with AI could qualify for significantly higher salary bands. The development reflects a wider trend across the tech industry, where companies are embracing AI while simultaneously reducing headcount.

title: Goa Fest 2026 Day 4: In the Age Of Artificial Intelligence, Originality Is Way Through
description: The article argues that AI will soon outperform humans in routine creative output, making originality and authorship the true differentiators. As execution becomes automated and uniformity rises, human value shifts toward taste, instinct, and unique perspective. The key takeaway from Goafest 2026 is to stop imitating patterns and focus on distinct, original expression.
content:
We keep worrying about: what happens when AI can do what we do?
But what we should be really worried about is: What then happens when AI becomes extremely good at doing, what everyone else is already doing?

title: MICHELLE GOLDBERG: The generation that grew up with AI hates it
description: When Eric Schmidt, former CEO of Google, started talking about artificial intelligence during a commencement speech at the University of Arizona last week, the graduates erupted in boos. "AI is going to touch everything," said Schmidt, as his stadium-sized audience roared its disapproval. "Whatever path you choose, AI will become part of how work is done." Maybe he meant this as a promise of opportunity, but the students seemed to hear it as a threat--or a curse.
